title: Eora cheesemanii
taxonomy: Eora cheesemanii is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The classification of Eora cheesemanii includes being a member of the tribe Genisteae. The species is further classified as belonging to the genus Eora, which is sometimes considered a synonym of Carmichaelia. As a result, Eora cheesemanii is also referred to as Carmichaelia cheesemanii by some botanical sources.
uses: Eora cheesemanii, a species native to New Zealand, has been used in traditional Māori medicine. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies are required to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal purposes.

parts: Eora cheesemanii is a plant native to New Zealand and it is also known as cheeseman's pepper or Cheeseman's pepper tree.  Eora cheesemanii has been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and berries are considered to be medicinal parts. The leaves are used to treat fever, while the berries are used to treat colds and sore throats. Eora cheesemanii also has antimicrobial properties, which may help to combat infections.
